sections.heading: Fentanyl Rehab in Des Moines: Key Facts

items.text: Fentanyl is a synthetic opioid up to 50x stronger than heroin, making withdrawal intense and relapse risk high
Medical detox is strongly recommended to manage severe symptoms and reduce overdose risk
We provide 24/7 supervised fentanyl detox with medication-assisted treatment (MAT) when appropriate
Continued care on-site with residential, PHP, and IOP for long-term recovery support
Treatment addresses co-occurring mental health disorders (dual diagnosis)
Most Insurances accepted. Same-day admissions available.
Located in Des Moines with easy access across Central Iowa.

sections.heading: Let Your Insurance Support Your Recovery
body: The cost of treatment should never be a barrier to the care you deserve. We are here to help you navigate your coverage so you can focus entirely on your recovery. Our team specializes in maximizing private insurance benefits to ensure your clinical stay at our Des Moines drug rehab is fully supported. Call us today to verify your benefits. 100% free.

sections.heading: Common Red Flags of Fentanyl Use
sections.subheading: Knowing When It Is Time for Help
body: Fentanyl is a synthetic opioid far more potent than drugs like heroin or morphine. Even small amounts can slow or stop breathing, making it a leading cause of overdose deaths in the U.S. It is often mixed into other drugs, including cocaine and counterfeit pills significantly increasing the risk of accidental overdose. According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention , synthetic opioids like fentanyl now drive the majority of opioid-related deaths nationwide. Common signs and symptoms of fentanyl use include:

items.text: Extreme drowsiness or “nodding off”
Slowed or shallow breathing
Pinpoint (constricted) pupils
Confusion or impaired thinking
Mood changes (euphoria, irritability or depression)
Strong cravings and drug-seeking behavior
Loss of interest in responsibilities or daily activities
Increased tolerance and dependence

sections.heading: Medical Fentanyl Detox in Des Moines
body: More than the high risk of overdose when using, attempting to stop fentanyl use on your own can be, in many cases, dangerous. Withdrawal can begin quickly and bring intense symptoms like nausea, insomnia, anxiety, and severe cravings that are difficult to manage alone. This often leads to early relapse, which is especially risky as tolerance drops rapidly, increasing the chance of overdose. Medical detox provides a controlled, supervised setting where symptoms are safely managed and stabilized, making it a critical first step toward recovery.

At Des Moines Wellness, we focus on making fentanyl detox as safe and manageable as possible so you don’t have to go through withdrawal alone. In our Polk County facility, we address both the physical and mental challenges of opioid withdrawal, including:

items.title: Physical Stabilization
items.text: Relieving symptoms like muscle pain, nausea, sweating, and flu-like discomfort during early withdrawal.

items.title: Sleep & Anxiety Support
items.text: Helping regulate insomnia, restlessness, and intense anxiety that often occur during detox.

items.title: Medical Monitoring
items.text: 24/7 supervision to track vital signs, manage complications, and ensure a safe detox process.

items.title: Cravings & Relapse Prevention
items.text: Using evidence-based methods, including MAT when appropriate, to reduce cravings and early relapse risk.

sections.heading: Fixing the Roots of Alcohol Addiction in Des Moines, Iowa
body: Detox is only the first step in fentanyl addiction treatment. Without continued support, the risk of relapse remains high. A full continuum of care provides the structure and clinical guidance needed to build long-term stability after detox. At Des Moines Wellness Center, individuals can transition seamlessly into the next level of care based on their needs, We offer:

items.title: Residential Inpatient
items.text: Immersive Stabilization — 24/7 structured care focused on therapy, and relapse prevention in a supportive environment.

items.title: Outpatient (PHP & IOP)
items.text: Back to Real Life — Flexible treatment options that provide ongoing clinical support while allowing a return to daily responsibilities.

items.title: Clinical Aftercare & Alumni
items.text: Staying Sober Long-Term — Continued guidance and relapse prevention planning to help maintain long-term recovery after treatment.

sections.heading: Fentanyl Addiction and Dual Diagnosis
body: Fentanyl addiction is often closely linked to underlying mental health conditions such as anxiety, depression, PTSD, or trauma-related disorders. This is called a dual diagnosis. Many individuals use opioids to cope with emotional distress, which can quickly lead to dependence and make recovery more complex. When both substance use and mental health disorders are present, treating only the addiction is rarely enough. Without addressing the underlying psychological factors, the risk of relapse remains significantly higher.

Dual diagnosis treatment focuses on both conditions at the same time through an integrated approach. This includes clinical evaluation, evidence-based therapies, and ongoing mental health support to help stabilize mood and build healthier coping mechanisms. By treating the root causes alongside fentanyl addiction, individuals are better equipped to achieve and maintain long-term recovery.

sections.heading: Job Protection and FMLA for Fentanyl Addiction Treatment
body: Many people delay getting help for fentanyl addiction because they’re worried about losing their job. In reality, federal protections may allow you to take time off for treatment while keeping your employment secure. The Family and Medical Leave Act ( FMLA ) allows eligible employees to take up to 12 weeks of unpaid, job-protected leave for serious health conditions, including substance use disorders. During this time, your position (or an equivalent one) is protected, and your health benefits may continue. In addition, seeking treatment is confidential. Employers do not receive details about your medical condition, only that leave has been approved when applicable.
Our admissions team can help guide you through the process, including coordinating documentation and answering questions about taking leave for treatment.

sections.heading: Fentanyl & Addiction Resources
body: Access to trusted resources can make a critical difference when facing fentanyl addiction. In addition to professional treatment, families have access to local and national organizations that provide overdose prevention and education. We advise:

items.title: Naloxone Iowa
items.text: Naloxone Iowa : Statewide initiative providing free naloxone (Narcan), overdose prevention education, and access to life-saving resources.

items.title: SAMHSA
items.text: SAMHSA: 24/7 confidential helpline (1-800-662-HELP) offering education, and support across the U.S.

items.title: National Institute on Drug Abuse (NIDA)
items.text: National Institute on Drug Abuse (NIDA) Federal research institute providing science-based information on fentanyl, opioid addiction, treatment approaches, and the long-term effects of substance use.

sections.heading: Medically Reviewed By Wesley Starlin, LMHC
body: Wesley Starlin is the Executive Director at Des Moines Wellness Center with over 15 years of experience in behavioral health and addiction treatment.

sections.heading: We’re Here to Listen When You’re Ready
body: Taking the first step is often the hardest, but you don’t have to do it by yourself. Whether you have a quick question about our Des Moines rehab or just need to hear a supportive voice, our team is available 24/7. We provide a safe, confidential space to discuss your options, verify your private insurance, and help you find the right path forward.

sections.heading: Fentanyl Addiction Treatment in Des Moines, Iowa
sections.subheading: Get safe, physician-led treatment for fentanyl addiction
body: Des Moines Wellness Center provides fentanyl addiction treatment in Des Moines with a full continuum of care, including medical detox, residential treatment, partial hospitalization (PHP) , and intensive outpatient programs (IOP) . Care is structured to address opioid dependence, withdrawal management, relapse prevention, and co-occurring mental health disorders.

As part of Quadrant Health Group , our programs are built on years of clinical experience and evidence-based practices, allowing clients to move through each level of care in one place without gaps in treatment. Located in Des Moines and serving communities across Iowa, our fentanyl rehab provides accessible fentanyl addiction treatment with convenient access from I-35, I-80, and I-235, and close proximity to Des Moines International Airport. We accept most insurance plans and offer same-day admissions when appropriate. If you or a loved one needs fentanyl addiction treatment in Des Moines, our team is available to help you take the first step. Don’t wait any more.

sections.heading: Let’s Build a Life You Don’t Need to Escape
body: Don’t let the weight of addiction decide your future. Whether you are seeking help for yourself or a loved one, our Des Moines admissions team is ready to provide the direct answers you need to move forward. We conduct a professional clinical assessment to find the best program for your needs while securing your private insurance benefits and job protection. Fill out the form below to get started, and a member of the team will reach out shortly.
